input: Generate RDS module for testdb09-dev PostgreSQL database. System Name: testdb09, Environment: dev, Owner: akshaya.durgapu@accenture.com. Upstream: init module outputs available. Requirements: Single-AZ (cost optimization), 20GB storage, daily automated backups. Security: encrypt with init KMS key, store password in Secrets Manager.
context: Init module provides vpc_id, kms_key_arn, db_subnet_group. Database names: pgvector. Tags: {Project: VectorDB, CostCenter: Engineering}
eval_notes: Verify: 1) RDS uses dev settings (db.t3.medium, backup_retention=7), 2) output includes endpoint + secret ARN, 3) naming follows pattern.

input: Generate Terraform for production EKS cluster on testdb09-prod. System: testdb09, Env: prod, Owner: akshaya.durgapu@accenture.com, Region: us-east-1. Upstream: init + bastion outputs. Requirements: 5-node cluster (HA), private node group, public node group (1-2 nodes), gp3 storage (EBS CSI), OIDC provider for IRSA, encrypted logs. Security: cluster endpoints private, SSH key from init, bastion role in cluster access.
context: Init: vpc_id, private_subnets=[3], public_subnets=[3], kms_key_arn, ssh_key_name. Bastion: role_arn, role_name. Kubernetes v1.32.
eval_notes: Check: 1) Bastion role in EKS access, 2) Node sizing (m5.xlarge prod, t3.xlarge dev), 3) OIDC URL extracted, 4) all logs encrypted, 5) security groups restrict remote_access.

input: Generate bastion host module for testdb09-dev. System: testdb09, Env: dev, Owner: akshaya.durgapu@accenture.com. Upstream: init outputs (vpc, subnets, kms, ssh key). Requirements: Single EC2 instance (t3.medium), RHEL 8, public IP + Elastic IP, CloudWatch agent, IAM role for parameter store access, security group allowing SSH from 0.0.0.0/0 (justify).
context: Init outputs: vpc_id, public_subnets, kms_key_arn, ssh_key_name, region=us-east-1. Bastion will be jump host for RDS/EKS access.
must_include: outputs include: public_ip, private_ip, security_group_id, role_arn
eval_notes: Verify: 1) SSH rule has tfsec comment, 2) IAM role only has s3:GetObject for logs, 3) user_data not hardcoded (use template), 4) Elastic IP attached, 5) instance uses init ssh_key_name.

input: Generate init module for us-east-1 test infrastructure. Requirements: 1 VPC (10.0.0.0/16), 3 AZs, private subnets (10.0.1.0/24, 10.0.2.0/24, 10.0.3.0/24), public subnets (10.0.11.0/24, 10.0.12.0/24, 10.0.13.0/24), KMS key for EBS/RDS/logs, VPC endpoints (S3, DynamoDB), private DNS, NAT instance. Config: platform_output (testdb09, dev), region us-east-1.
context: Foundation for all other modules. Will be referenced by EKS, RDS, Bastion. Outputs: vpc_id, subnets, kms_key_arn, ssh_key_name, db_subnet_group, private/public zone IDs.
must_include: outputs: vpc_id, private_subnets, public_subnets, kms_key_arn, dns_private_zone_id
eval_notes: Check: 1) 3 AZs with correct subnets, 2) NAT gw in one AZ, 3) S3/DynamoDB endpoints, 4) KMS rotation enabled, 5) Private hosted zone for internal DNS, 6) output structure matches downstream expectations.

input: Generate PostgreSQL + pgvector schema module for testdb09-dev. Upstream: RDS output (endpoint, port, username, password secret ARN). Requirements: Create pgvector extension, init schema (tables: documents, embeddings), IAM auth, sample migration script. Use Helm for Weaviate connection.
context: RDS module provides: db_endpoint, db_port, db_username, db_password (in Secrets Manager). K8s cluster has postgres-operator or similar. Environment: dev.
eval_notes: This is a bridge module. Check: 1) Reads password from Secrets Manager ARN, 2) Uses local-exec to run psql (or null_resource), 3) pgvector enabled, 4) Outputs schema name for downstream.

input: [VALIDATION TEST] Review this EKS module. Identify security issues and suggest fixes.
context: User provides EKS TF code with: cluster endpoint public, IAM role has wildcard actions, security group allows 0.0.0.0/0 all traffic, no KMS encryption, no OIDC.
eval_notes: Review accuracy: All 4 issues identified? Fixes align with org standards? Suggestions are actionable?

input: Optimize this dev EKS cluster for cost while maintaining functionality. Current: 5 nodes m5.xlarge, 90-day log retention, gp3 volumes (500 IOPS).
context: Environment: dev, workload: learning/testing, not production.
must_not_include: removal of HA features in prod
eval_notes: Verify: 1) Savings calculation correct? 2) Dev environment optimization doesn't break prod? 3) Conditional logic preserves isolation?

input: Validate these 4 modules for cross-module consistency: init, bastion, eks, rds. Check: variable names match output variable names? Outputs follow mpp_report pattern? Dependency chain is acyclic?
context: User provides outputs.tf from init, bastion, eks, rds modules.
eval_notes: Check for: 1) Type mismatch (string vs list)? 2) Missing intermediate outputs? 3) Circular dependencies?

input: Generate Terraform for Weaviate Helm deployment on EKS. Upstream: EKS output (endpoint, kubeconfig, cluster name), RDS output (pgvector endpoint optional). Requirements: Helm release (chart: weaviate, version: 17.7.0), 3 replicas, persistent storage (gp3 10Gi), API key secrets, gRPC enabled, LoadBalancer service for external access.
context: K8s provider configured with EKS cluster. Namespace: weaviate. Service account with IRSA role for backups (S3).
eval_notes: Check: 1) Helm repo registered? 2) Namespace isolation? 3) IRSA role attached? 4) PVs use gp3 StorageClass? 5) Service endpoints exported?
